macro.title: Positive housing demand leads to inventory almost going negative YOY
macro.what: Pending sales rose to 79,220 vs 74,212 last year as rates dipped to 6.42% and inventory growth slowed to 1.49% year over year.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: The Price Point: What $250,000 Buys in These Best Cities for New Grads
housing.what: In this edition of The Price Point—a weekly series where Redfin data journalists explore current housing inventory across a rotating combination of budgets and metro areas—we’re shifting our focus toward a few of the best cities for new college grads, according to Redfin’s latest report on the subject (conducted in partnership with Glassdoor)
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.title: Just Listed in Texas: A $12 Million Lakefront Estate That’s Luxurious Inside and Out
housing.what: In this edition of Just Listed–a series from Redfin News that explores listings that are listed by Redfin or Compass agents on Redfin.com–we’re exploring a Mediterranean-inspired estate in Austin, Texas
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.
